channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: both Swift and DTCC are going live right now — 177 replies — 708.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
both Swift and DTCC are going live right now

Open thread • Replies: 177 • Updated: 2026-07-18 13:43 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62466455
Price?

62466457
>>62466455
Suppressed,

62466461
And literal top-buying turned full time fudders are finally capitulating.

Imagine subjecting yourself to all the pain and never seeing it through the end. I gave myself 6 months after SWIFT and DTCC are live to see if the token shoots up, and then decide wether to sell half my stack of just staking rewards.

But to sell RIGHT BEFORE the literal ANNOUNCED DATES of the two biggest financial behemoths' launches? That's crazy

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62488229
>>62488211
fuck you bastard bich

62488315
>>62472871
>(PA version 2 is said to automate this)
payment abstraction is doing this. Its just changing some shit around from uniswap to cow swap and adding a dutch auction. it does nothing to adress the buying tokens problem

62488326
>>62488315
>buying tokens on the open market does not address the buying tokens problem

?

62488334
>>62488326
sir why is link at 8 dollars my village is hungry. Big partnership dtcc will pump it right?

62488492
>>62488326
they are outpacing the buys with dumps. you still are dancing around that you absolute imbecile

62488527
>>62488492
All cryptos do that. They all have inflation that far outstrips usage fees.

62488547
>>62488527
how much are you getting paid to post on here? there is no way you are just here saying the same wrong shit over and over again and believe it

62488569
>>62488547
>how much are you getting paid to post on here?

says the faggot blaming Chainlink specifically for the most standard crypto behaviors

62488802
>>62488569
please the village is hungry

62489225
>>62466449
That's cool and all... unfortunately it has zero impact on the token price, so who the fuck cares that Sergey and his minions will rake in another billion in the future.

62492292
>>62486482
>they are pumping bitcoin

62492353
>>62466449
What exactly "went live" here? Was it used for anything? Is there any suggestion that this most recent round of techno-jibbajabba has seen any application?

62492372
>>62492353
https://www.dtcc.com/news/2026/july/15/dtcc-turns-tokenization-into-reality

"successfully converted assets held at The Depository Trust Company (DTC) into tokens that were then used in real production trades"

"The event featured several transactions made across various asset classes in a DTC production environment, including collateral pledge, security lending, U.S. Treasury/repo delivery-versus-payment (DVP) trade, equity DVP trade, equity delivery-versus-delivery (DVD) trade, equity token transfer and central counterparty (CCP) margin workflows."

62492759
>>62492372
one-time pilot, got it
>inb4 now wait for October bro

62492898
>>62492759
someone's salty his coin wasn't involved.

62496373
Chainlink CRE did the collateral workflows... big

62496378
>>62496373
p+c

62496397
>>62496378
going up

62496401
>>62496397
in your dreams, stinkie
in your pathetic, loser-filled dreams

62496520
>>62492759
Yeah that's a fucken demo. Not a production use case.

62496533
>>62492372
>deeeemooooooo
whats that i hear?
>deeeeemoooooooo
it's the demo bell~
>deeeeeemoooooooooo
it means 5 more years of waiting until maybe real production use :}

62496544
>>62496373
>Hey guys, hear me out. What if we pretended that chopping up "assets" into fractional pieces was some sort of world changing idea? Like, you could chop up a loan into 50,000 pieces. How cool would THAT be?? It serves a very very real and practical purpose in the financial world. You will see.
Any actually useful financial vehicle is proposed and adopted almost immediately in the real world. Tokenization is just air sold to retards to keep the grift going just a little longer.

62496564
>deeeeeemoooooooooooo

62496570
>>62496401
Ok Nico.

62496684
>>62496520
Do you really think getting into a semantic debate with DTCC's wording is going to make you feel better about XRP not being involved at all?

62496690
Okay they've been live for a week, where's my $1000 LINK? Or even just my $100 LINK? Or perhaps even just my $10 LINK?

62496743
>>62496684
I don't invest in any crypto whatsoever. Every inch of it is for retards.

62496998
>>62496743
>Every inch of it is for retards.
Yeah, retards like DTCC, Swift, JP Morgan, Blackrock, Linux Foundation, Microsoft, Nasdaq, NYSE, S&P Dow Jones Indices, the US Department of Commerce, and many many more.

62497078
>>62496544
You’re arguing against fractionalization, not tokenization. Fractional ownership has existed for decades through REITs, ETFs, ABS, and syndicated loans. Tokenization isn’t claiming to invent that. It’s an attempt to modernize the ownership and settlement rails. Whether it succeeds is an empirical question, not one answered by pointing out that fractionalization already exists. Many projects are hype, but that doesn’t make the underlying infrastructure worthless.

62497218
>>62496998
>Yeah, retards like DTCC, Swift, JP Morgan, Blackrock, Linux Foundation, Microsoft, Nasdaq, NYSE, S&P Dow Jones Indices, the US Department of Commerce, and many many more.
Sure sure. They've all "expressed interest" or "have a team", but none of them are actually using it. It's just lip service to keep their fingers in many pies. I'll say it again, this is 100% useless, is going nowhere and is being used ONLY as star trek gibberish to keep greater fools on the hook. Using a public ledger (which is all crypto brings to the table) to do any of these things is utterly pointless when it can all be done via traditional digital systems at about 100000x the speed.

item.title: As I was saying yesterday. The Chinese are slowly giving the news to trading partners. No more dollars. You want to buy… — 49 replies — 134.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
As I was saying yesterday. The Chinese are slowly giving the news to trading partners. No more dollars. You want to buy…

Open thread • Replies: 49 • Updated: 2026-07-18 13:35 UTC

OP

As I was saying yesterday. The Chinese are slowly giving the news to trading partners. No more dollars. You want to buy from us. You get a Yuan swap line. You settle trade imbalances in gold or Yuan and use our settlement systems.

What can the US do? All they have is threats against the likes of Argentina. China has the vital goods they need at the right prices. The will continue to do this and kill dollar dominance.

Theres no other choice or enter an inflationary spiral.

Early replies (first 3)

62488083
how does this play out in 20 years? half the countries buy using Yuan? US goes back to manufacturing cuz dollar falls? Americans finally lose weight?

62488086
>>62488026
I thought Milei was anti-Chinese?

62488137
>Argentine peso devalues to zimbabwe-tier levels
>Hard for businesses to even buy USD because the market is so shallow and the currency so unwanted price discovery becomes tricky
>Milei's lolbertarian stance does little to appease markets, forex remains tightly regulated to avoid massive capital flights
>country with almost no industry or advanced services to export
>USD reserves run dry
>Great opportunity for China to dump it's surplus production, will probably be selling shit to Argentina at a loss in a government-set artificial exchange rate just as they do with Veneuela and African shitholes that struggle to access USD
>brown thirdie posts his paid shilling painting this as a massive victory for china and a sign of the dollar collapsing
We should have flags on /biz/. I'd call you a chink but I don't think chinks can even access this site.
>As I was saying yesterday.
Your thread died within 24hs on one of the slowest boards on this site. That should tell you something. Fuck off paid brown tankie.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62493654
>>62493633
Yeah Im buying PMs

62493820
>>62493633
>bitcoin favored by free societies
>gold favored by centralized dragon hoarders
Looks like gold is going to be the underlying settlement asset of choice for all parties involved dessssu (at least in the near term)
I say this as a btc/xmr maxi

62493870
>>62493633
>pegging our currency to Saylor Moon and leverage algorithm jews

62495427
>>62493633
top signal

62495485
>>62493550
>Patagonia
That's at least true or only the usual Internet legend? I've seen plenty of rumors and accusations, but so far nothing substantial.

62495495
>>62488302
Is China back on the gold standard?

62495499
>>62493550
Are Argentinians actually worse off now compared to a decade or two or three decades ago?

62495510
>>62488026
>fastforward 18 months
>the usa has enacted a special operation to kill the argentinian government

62495529
>>62488137
>Great opportunity for China to dump it's surplus production, will probably be selling shit to Argentina at a loss in a government-set artificial exchange rate just as they do with Veneuela and African shitholes that struggle to access USD
This. China's economic slump is currently so bad they 'reported' the slowest growth in a generation.

62495698
>>62495510
Basically the same thing as israel and hamas
Always follow the money

62495764
Why would China want more demand for the yuan? They intentionally drive the demand for it down so that their exports benefit.

62495806
You own the YUAN?
Communism out here selling dreams like a busted dude promising everybody a mansion while he stealing the fridge out the kitchen. Every single time they talk about “everybody equal,” somehow the regular people end up broke, hungry, standing in lines for bread while the top bosses chilling fat behind locked gates acting like kings.
They be saying, “share everything,” but what it really mean is: “you own nothing, the government own YOU.” Wanna speak your mind? Nah. Wanna worship freely? Nah. Wanna disagree? Congratulations, now you suspicious. Whole system move like a control freak ex that gotta monitor every little thing you do.
And the economy? Absolute clown show. Folks working hard just to get empty shelves and busted apartments while propaganda posters smiling harder than the actual citizens. History got receipts too — censorship, secret police, people disappearing, leaders acting untouchable while preaching “for the people” every five minutes.
That’s the wild part about communism: it start sounding like a superhero speech and end looking like a prison with extra paperwork. They promise equality, then everybody equally miserable except the dudes running the show.
At some point you gotta ask: if the system so perfect, why people always trying to escape it instead of rushing in?

62495971
>>62488026
but what can you do with yuan, other than buy gold? who the fuck wants to live in china where saying "i think the government might be slightly incorrect" gets you a lifetime of jail? who wants to live around han chinese people who see everyone who isn't them as less than human? who the fuck wants to learn god damn mandarin? the yuan will never be as actually valuable as the dollar because nobody gets rich to move to china, you get rich to move to America.

62496058
>>62488330
Who do you mean by "you"? I'm American. And America isn't going to nuke anyone. America is going to be cut off at the knees financially and will have no choice but to issue its own gold-backed trade currency if it wants to stay in the game. And that will put an end to the party.

62496102
>>62495495
Not yet, but the signs point to them issuing a gold-backed trade currency.

62496108
>>62495764
Global demand for the dollar as trade and reserve currency was an incredibly lucrative financial arrangement for the USA for decades. It allowed massive money creation without much inflation. China wants that. They have said it publicly.

62496111
Why did we get $40 billion to Argentina again?

62496112
>>62495971
You don't have to live in America to use the dollar. Why would you have to live in China to use the Yuan?

62496252
>>62496108
No kidding but the US’s status as the world reserve currency is pretty unique; it wasn’t exactly an organic happening. Going from a gold-backed reserve currency to a fiat reserve currency has a lot of benefits, no doubt, especially since US debt is denominated in dollars. Still, China isn’t even close to having a freely floating currency, and their dominance on the global stage still comes from their export advantage. I’m not saying that they don’t want the position, I’m just saying it’s probably premature to claim they’re going for it.

62496284
>>62488026
>What can the US do?
Nothing, all this was planned by jewry for decades. Everybody with 2 braincells knew it would be the inevitable outcome ever since the mass migration of (((industrial capital))) started in the 90s. They fucking dismantled every factory in the US to build it back in China for 25 years straight, selling you le service economy & le chink slave until the end times bullshit fantasy.
End result : 98 millions jobs destroyed, ultra vulnerable and dependant on all kinds of strategic resources, no industrial capacity, rampant inflation, reduced diplomatic influence, destroyed or terminally old energy/transport/etc... infrastructure.

It's already over, the jews are physically leaving the boat, there is nothing left to loop or vampirize from the US. Only one rug on their financial scamfest and the whole house of card collapse.

62496870
>>62495510
>It's raining on Buenos Aires

62496874
>>62495806
Now try sounding 20% less like a nigger, ChatGPT

62496966
>>62495806
Finally an intelligent person in this thread. I’m so tired of non-americans. Thank God for Elon Musk and Donald Trump.

62497156
>>62488026
>paying for imports in the currency of the exporting nation
Hey look it's fucking nothing.

62497161
>>62493633
>the solution is my bags
God I hate you bitcoin morons

62497162
Oh no another person saying the dollar is dying

I have only been hearing this daily for twenty years now

62497163
>>62495499

62497189
>>62488086
Anti-china stances in the region are more for propaganda consumption. In reality most try to find a balance playing the US and China off each other

62497196
>>62497161
their bag seems to be the solution to every problem

62497206
>>62496058
Hilariously, the country most probable to get nuked is the US... by nuking itself.

item.title: The older I get, the more I realize that NEETs are right about everything. — 20 replies — 80.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
The older I get, the more I realize that NEETs are right about everything.

Open thread • Replies: 20 • Updated: 2026-07-18 13:56 UTC

OP

The older I get, the more I realize that NEETs are right about everything. I was a reasonably successful programmer who got laid off and I haven't been able to find work in almost a year. My family has been burning through savings like crazy. Meanwhile my brother is a NEET who seems to have experienced 0.1% of the hardship I've gone through. He's always cheerful and spends most of his time socializing or playing video games. It feels like he somehow perceived the stakes of the modern world correctly, while I fucked up by following the standard cultural script. NEET really is the correct move on the game board if you can pull it off and if you're able to see it. Society must have completely unraveled for this to be true, but it is.

Early replies (first 3)

62496918
Demoralization thread

Arbeit macht frei

62496920
>OP is a faggot and will never be a woman

62496925
neeting is fun if you have a broad range of interests and hobbies
just rotting away in your mom's basement is worse than being a wagie

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62496927
>>62496925
Rotting away in a basement (ie playing games all day) is better than being in my situation (wagie with a family, potentially permanently underemployed, doomed to a life of underclass toil).

62496934
>>62496918
if programming is "arbeit" why do they all look like this XD XD XD

62496938
nothing stop you from joining your brother and NEETing

62496940
>>62496938
I have a family now. Besides, my parents couldn't afford to support another person.

62496949
>>62496940
>I have a family now. Besides, my parents couldn't afford to support another person.

grim

62496952
>>62496949
It is what it is. Both my parents are working class. I moved Heaven and Earth to get a solid middle class life, but I guess it wasn't meant to be.

62497138
I want to NEET, but because I have money saved and invested I am not entitled to gibs.

62497140
>>62496912
>My family has been burning through savings like crazy.
he fell for the "don't make the kids panic, we have to keep sending like normal and pretending everything is normal" meme.

62497150
>>62496912
>My family
I'm sorry, am I supposed to believe a sexhaver is capable of self-reflection?

62497214
>>62497138
If you move your investments into illiquid assets of some kind, would they still expect you to liquidate to pay for your life?

>>62497140
Not really, kids are just expensive.

>>62497150
I have sex like three times a year anon.

62497219
>>62496912
I spent almost a decade NEETing. If you don't have money to travel, even hobo-style, then you will just rot away. If you have some decent income and can stay fit and you are able to travel and meet girls, then yeah NEETing can be fun.
>>62496927
>playing games all day
You get bored of it, especially past the age of 30. I was so bored of my life that I became a wagie out of boredom.

62497224
>>62497219
What do you mean by rotting away

62497231
>>62497214
>I have sex like three times a year anon
I'm sorry bro

62497232
>>62497219
What do you do for work?

62497234
>>62497231
Thank you anon

62497238
>>62496912
and where does your brother get money from?

62497245
>>62497238
Parents and "disability".

item.title: Most notable reddit investment factions — 8 replies — 32.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Most notable reddit investment factions

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-07-18 13:56 UTC

OP

I have lurked in reddit for like 3 days and i found 3 notable investment factions that hate each other.

1. WSB - you already know this homos.
2. Bogleheads - Boomers and millenials, won't call them retarded.
3. Dividend gangs
Interesting people. They hate 1 and 2.
They think buying voo is gambling and is similar to bitcoins because they rely on future price changing.
They want to own stale on free cahsflow and get bennies,
and reinvest bennies to get more bennies.
They say if you need to sell your stonks after you retire you are wrong.

How do you guys think about 3?
They seem kind of heterodox.

Early replies (first 3)

62496098
>>62496092
>2. Bogleheads - Boomers and millenials, won't call them retarded.
They're retarded also when we say millennials who the fuck are we talking about, because this board used to be 90% millennials gambling on crypto in 2017. Do we mean "millennial" (born 1981) or millennial (born 1993)?

62496113
>>62496098
Their bogle people seem to have more older millenials, but not sure.
I think they also have some gen zs as some people keep asking what to do with their small wages.

62496115
>>62496113
>le career progression meme
I'm out.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62496714
cash crew #1
everyone hates us
CASH CREW REPRESENT

62496720
>>62496714
Fuck yeah bro lets hijack this thead for the cash crew

62496816
i go to plebbit normie financial advice reddits and be the only only one telling these ETF idiots to buy gold and silver (a good safe too which in itself is a good investement). They sperg, mald and cope everytime.

62497237
>>62496092
3 sounds absolutely retarded low T and risk adverse. If you think VOO is too risky you need to blast tren to save your nutless bag between your legs. You also have to consider what the alternative to NOT investing is, which is sitting in fiat, which is destined to go down no matter what from our money printing usurious economic system. So not only are they low T, risk adverse losers, they are also retarded.

62497244
>>62496816
>saaar pls buy my bags at cyclical decade highs saaaaaaaaaar
Indian housewife detected.

item.title: Day 641 Of Bobo Rekt in Anus Posting Until BTC is at 180K — 3 replies — 8.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Day 641 Of Bobo Rekt in Anus Posting Until BTC is at 180K

Open thread • Replies: 3 • Updated: 2026-07-18 13:36 UTC

OP

Failing hand torch caught
Holding high in new cycle
Bottoming stands close

Early replies (first 3)

62496727
>>62496701
This wont happen until the next halving, but on that day it will be guaranteed, so no need to post this

62497201
>>62496701

62497207
W haiku

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: SPACEX IS ENGINEERING A HUGE PRICE DUMP ON PURPOSE — 8 replies — 8.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
SPACEX IS ENGINEERING A HUGE PRICE DUMP ON PURPOSE

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-07-18 13:00 UTC

OP

Post-IPO Spacex now has over $100bn in cash. They have at LEAST five years of capex in the bank so the share price means fuck all to them.

They're gonna dump the price right into the Q2 earnings shareholder unlock with an intentionally bungled Starship launch so institutions can DCA their cost basis down to a reasonable level.

>Starship can currently launch about once a month

They can run this scam with every unlock tranche if they want to.

Early replies (first 3)

62491806
>>62491795
Spacex is gaysex.

62491824
Space sex

62491926
This happens in tech when they are gearing up for a new technology release. If you get caught investing in the old tech you fall behind. Everyone rushing to put these new chips into robots and god knows what else. I personally despise elon musk and tesla vehicles.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62496195
>>62491795
so you say they're dumping on purpose
when janitors sell, they scoop them up at a low price
then ride it back up when they unveil AGI and space gold
nice evil villain plot line

62496355
spaceKEKS

62496398
Spacex is unironically the next big thing once it bottoms out.
>hits 20 bucks a share or something in the 2028 bear market
>runs back to 200 and then 400 as President Newsom votes for moon landings or some shit in 2030
>sneedlon is a trillionaire

62496579
>>62491795
elon is cashing out on his dumbass believers before he's forgotten after trump's presidency ends

62497151
>>62491795
first post-IPO lockup on insiders expires this august.
more than 900 million shares will bee freed and will start circulating the market (more than double than the currently available shares)
if people believe spacex is dumping now, they'll be amazed by what happens in august.
that's what you get for investing in overhyped and overpriced stocks with such tiny float.

As dancing Israelis smile

item.title: Everest MiCA CASP — 118 replies — 35.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Everest MiCA CASP

Open thread • Replies: 118 • Updated: 2026-07-18 10:37 UTC

OP

I want to personally thank every Everest ID hater in here, talking to you Link baggies. They secured MiCA CASP last week.

A single MFSA license is valid across 27 EU states, reaching ~450M people. Of the ~283 CASPs on the ESMA register, nearly all are exchanges or brokers. Everest is one of the rare licensed infrastructure providers: identity and payment rails, not another venue.

Enjoy it furries

Early replies (first 3)

62461998
>>62461988
I thought it would have mooned harder from the news. What gives

62462006
It is because we are meant to suffer as token holders just like the Linkies

62463131
>Unlisted from coingecko
Lmao!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62487944
Go look at marketcap of other tokens with MiCA CASP that Binance could not get lol

62488146
>>62487917
100k sui 400k make it

62488344
We need one of the holders to go through a humiliation ritual, I think that’s when it will start running and never return to earth again

62489235
>>62470842
good. fuck that guy
can't believe he turned his show into an Everest shill

62489843
Bob said he is a mix of Kobe and Tiger Woods the other week. I don’t know much about sports but I think those are very good players who won championships!

62490258
>>62489843
Nigger worshipping is bearish retard

62490777
He also said Tom Brady but those were only 3. I guess that evens it out

62490860
I am indian and i bought, sounds buliesh

62491099
Bob gave Lionel Messi a bath when Messi was a baby. Think about that for a second.

62492644
Good Morning Sherpas! Happy Friday as I take my morning parasite dump

62492894
>>62492644
just like the everest chart

62493912
>>62490777
Two niggers and a cuck. Project is cooked

62494198
Yea you are on crack, most competitive athletes each of those 3 sports likely ever had, let along how successful they were. Everest will be fine

62495727
Bob is the least atheletic cuck I have ever seen

62495846
Brad is LeBron James

62495855
Actually Brad is Prince Fielder

62495868
Pretty funny how a lot of folks in here buy XRP or Link expecting 1000x from billions in mc when ID is 2 mil mc with MiCA CASP to operate permanently in Europe. Go look at other companies that got it, much bigger than Everest. Bob has been preaching MiCA since 2022 in his newsletters, dude is living 4 years ahead of the present and you idiots don’t see it smfh amazing

62495961
So excited for you Kiddos...
The Oracle partnership is still alive and news incoming.

62496061
I doubt Oracle still a partner BUT I find it very weird Everchain went private then a few weeks later Oracle went live with the Financial Crime and Compliance Management Service that Everest said they partnered with them to build. The Oracle guy that Bob worked with left so maybe they found someone else for the job but still Everchain going private for security/privacy reasons in August 24 then Financial Crime and Compliance Management Service going live September 24 seemed like super weird coincidence. I did all the research I could, most I could find is third party is being used by Oracle but nothing more. Once again, doubt it’s Everest since Bobs contact left them but he never confirmed 100% no matter how much holders asked, holders also forgot and did not ask about Oracle until years later randomly lol

62496070
The Everest lore goes so fucking deep you autistics would be shocked kek, fuckers did pilot with Indonesia gov in 2019 where citizens with no device were able to receive government stipends then spend at grocery store all through Everest and their ID. If I explained every little thing and person Bob has gone through and met, you all would think this was billions in market cap like an XRP or XLM but literally sitting at zero down 99% from 2021 waiting for a couple dipshit whales to find it and send Big Green Dildos for next 2 decades

62496078
I encourage you 4 chan slop maxxers to dedicate 30 minutes of your ADHD attention span to read Bob’s Everest newsletters the last 6 years on Medium. You will then realize all of your shitcoin founders are pajeet retards that will rug or run out of funding these next few years

62496116
Charlie Munger at 200 years old once said: "There is an old German proverb I've always liked and it says, 'Man is too soon old and too late smart.'
Well retards the 10,000x is staring you right in the face so you do not have to wait until dead zombie mode to enjoy it. Like holy fuck could this be any more clear or obvious

62496134
>>62495961
You forgot to change id saar

62496187
I know you are not used to hearing this but please shut the heck up troll

62496329
Oracle is active.
There are no coincidences.
This is a banking product.
For Banks.

Who here can find me the Everest episode of Chainlink Tonight?

Just want to confirm the Everest webpage linked to a Dept of Defense info page.

62496405
>>62496329
Oracle contract was lost. Bob already said as much when he alluded to certain partnerships not working out

62496425
>>62496405
He never said they lost Oracle contract you fucking retard.
To think he wouldn't prioritize the biggest software company in the world is fucking retarded and you are a fucken retard.

62496426
>>62496425
Just stop coping and be happy he got Mica. Oracle is dead and all evidence proves that

62496769
>>62496426
another nail to the evergay casket

62496941
Just want to confirm the Everest webpage linked to a Dept of Defense info page.

This has been asked for before and it’s long gone just like Jace/Bob taco meet up footage Bob wanted deleted. Lots of hush hush kek, probably nothing burger as nothing good Everest happens here

item.title: AirFox — 55 replies — 22.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
AirFox

Open thread • Replies: 55 • Updated: 2026-07-18 11:33 UTC

OP

Any qrd on what’s going on with this thing? What exactly are these guys hoping to accomplish? Crypto is cooked and they keep trying to change this project in ways that don’t seem to matter. The TG link also doesn’t work anymore.

Early replies (first 3)

62481067
>>62480640
>2 hours later
>0 replies
jfc it’s worse than I thought. These threads used to reach bump limits after a couple hours.

62481088
>>62480640
it's a dead shitcoin, the devs are just waiting for the few final bagholders to give up so they can wrap it up and enjoy the money they made selling this shit

62481280
>>62480640
its just a rebrand and a relaunch with shifty eyes about robinhood instead of base.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62484716
>>62481088
They rebranded, increased token supply for themselves and airdropped everyone the new tokens.

62485941
>>62483861
>the fake pump did t even hold for 24 hours
I hope no one thought that mini pump was organic. It is just one of many that happened in the last couple of years and it’s probably a tg member trying to farm liquidity. It’s sad that this is what the volume is based on. Some bot / poorfag trying to farm $10-$50 from some other poorfags who got baited into buying a green chart lol

62486102
where is the fucking liquidity i cant sell what the fuck i need my money now. airfox? really? where is my cent per AVI that was promised to me

62486284
>>62486102
There is $11k liquidity. Thats more than enough

62486513
>$300k mcap
Yea I’m thinking it’s over

62486525
>>62486513
$80m whale is starting to sell. A former six figure bag is now worth $4k. Ouch

62487100
>>62484514
The Schizo also won and the red baron roped.
Flawless Victory

>>62481067
>>62481088
Anon, crypto is dead just as this board.
can't you fucking tell this already after two years?
The days of making 5 figs or even 3 figs on ETH shitcoins is over.
retail sadly migrated to solana and crime is legal now
those days of quick money on ETH and biz shitcoins are not coming back

62487573
>>62486525
a few bags were worth 7 figs at the height (yeah yeah, muh liquidity, I know) silly fuckers held all this time, that's gotta hurt, a few got out with 6 figs but fucked the chart

62487601
>>62486284
whoa, wtf? hadn't even spotted that, 9k now.....pffffffhahahahah

62487664
>>62487100
Solana is part of why crypto got fucked imo. Outside of the scammers and a few degens, I can’t imagine too many people making money there. But yea I don’t see any light at the end of the tunnel for crypto as a whole

62489045
Damn we hit $200k. Which one of you fucks is selling?

62489432
>>62481067
If you haven't noticed, anon, jannies and jeets wrung every drop of juice out of this board for years. I still come back on occasion and reply out of habit, just to find the majority of my posts are the end of a thread. /biz/ is a fucking ghost town

62489758
Wait a minute tho, if he does actually launch this token launcher etc for robin hood, it could be one of the early launches and go to the moon

62489887
>>62489432
>just to find the majority of my posts are the end of a thread
lol I thought it was just me. I’ll go into what seems like an active thread and as soon as I post a reply, that shit dies. It’s weird

>>62489758
>robinhood
I’m very skeptical about sentiment going back to what it was. I honestly don’t think crypto will ever be a space where we see 100x’s across the board. Even 2024s run was shit and I don’t think we will see that either. I see robinhood as another Base type of thing. Granted, Base did pretty good in the beginning but it ultimately turned out to be a chain for insider trading. That’s why crypto is in the state that it’s in. Nothing but a bunch of jews

62489964
>>62489045
>down 27% off less than 4k volume
yeah this shit is cooked.

62490304
>>62489887
>I see robinhood as another Base type of thing
I don’t even see it as that. Remember how Base was supposed to onboard 1b people? Remember how bad the on chain summer was? Coinbase was, and still is, one of the most well known names in the space and they couldn’t even pull it off. People stopped caring. I hope I’m wrong but all the things are leading to RH being another nothing burger

62490407
>>62487664
Yeah Scamlana fucked the shitcoin market and stole too much liquidity. And before that, a new useless ghost chain copy pasted version of ETH being created every week.
it's ogre for ETH and the shitcoin market overall
>T. Roundtripped a $110k Apu bag and held like a good baggie

62490814
>>62490407
>roundtripped
That’s the thing that worries me if we ever did have another bull run. The dumps were just as quick as the pumps and a lot of people were caught off guard. Too many baggies were created and many are still holding on, including whales. I see that across a lot of the stuff I’m in. So if we ever did have a run up I feel like the sell pressure would be worse than before because everyone that’s still holding will want to exit with whatever profits they can get. All the shitcoins with little liquidity are going to get nuked because of how pvp it will get. I’m a baggie who round tripped a mid-upper 6 figure bag across a bunch of projects and I can tell you right now that my standards of what I consider to be “making it” is far less than before. idgaf about making 7 figures anymore. Even getting 1/3 of my ATH is enough for me to exit and I’m sure a lot of people feel this way

62490948
>>62489045
>Which one of you fucks is selling?
lmao, you must be new here

62491616
I just noticed that 60m wallet that’s selling is an early wallet that has ties to paw / shib related stuff. I’m getting ‘frens and supporters’ vibes with this one. It’s likely the team knows who owns that wallet. It’s never going to end…..

62493068
and with that…avi’s story has come to a close. happy third birthday aviator. the final day . i am done. goodbye. 3 years too long. it’s not cozy anymore its just sad. i just cant do this anymore im sorry

62493393
>>62493068
>im out
The last sells were $48 and $4. That you?

62493444
>>62493393
no its not even worth it for me to go through the process of selling, im just done with the tg and having any hope at all for the team. its obvious they cannot find success in anything they make. sure they made stuff that works but nobody cares outside of 3 active users in tg. nothing can save this coin or the products, nobody cares. nobody uses the arcade, nobody uses the bridge. they fumbled every step of the way and it’s too far gone. stixil is vibe coding the stratus and it looks like everyone else left? we need a wellness check on pengo

62493696
>>62493444
I don’t know what is left of the team. It would be nice to at least know that much.
>no one cares
I agree but what narratives are left that people care about? It’s all been done before. It’s not even an issue with avi imo it’s the whole market. Most of the volume out there is fake. Things that seem to be doing good are really not. Wash trading and other manipulative tactics are used to make it look like crypto is alive. I believe stixil genuinely tried to make a legit project. Crypto is a tough space to build in and be successful. The ex-shib narrative was really the only thing they had going for it hem in regards to connections but ultimately it didn’t do anything.

62494825
>>62480640
I told you THIS SHIT WAS GOING TO GO TO ZERO.
WHY DID YOU NOT SELL? WHY?

62494980
WTFWT

62495070
>>62494825
I bought at $300k and my $100 has nearly doubled since. Cope and seethe tranny

62495627
make the price go up now. stixil you owe me that much atleast. let me exit with my pride.

62495664
>>62495627
The price is 2x from where it was yesterday. This is your last chance. Don’t get caught round tripping again

62497002
>>62495664
Check the price. The early friends and supporters strike again KEKW!

item.title: Who's still buying BTC at this level? — 62 replies — 19.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Who's still buying BTC at this level?

Open thread • Replies: 62 • Updated: 2026-07-18 10:48 UTC

OP

BTC has already come a long way. Are you still buying here, waiting for a pullback, or just holding what you already have?

>Curious what everyone's plan is.

Early replies (first 3)

62475243
waiting for $10k and I will buy 3 btc

62475257
>>62475235
YOUR MOTHER

62475258
>>62475235
I'm DCAing bitcoin at the second half of the midterm year.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62486528
>>62483293
DCAing into Bitcoin in the second half of a midterm year usually works out pretty well. Remember, just knowing we're in a bear market isn't enough, you have to take action. You aren't going to time the exact bottom, but slowly accumulating when prices are low is how you navigate bear markets.

62486684
>>62484717
TBVs are still in the public testnet phase on Aave V4. Hopefully we'll see a launch later this year.

62487150
>>62486684
It's a boring market

62487536
>>62487150
How do you know? It's still in testnet. They're literally testing the use cases. Plenty of potential if it works.

62488078
>>62486528
You're making good money.

62488091
>>62486462
Time to take profit

62488156
>>62475235
at 40k yeah

62488664
>>62487536
We'll see. More ways to use native BTC are what will matter in the long run.

62488668
>>62488156
not possible

62489527
>>62483295
New hype chain lol

62489716
>>62475235
ahem

62490257
>>62488664
Babylon's probably the best place to keep up with BTCFi right now.

62490309
>>62488156
not 39k? Lol

62491802
Lfg

62491816
Anything around the 200 week moving average is a bargain.

I'm DCAing heavily at these levels. I want to get rid of all my cash by October.

62492766
>>62490257
Hard not to when they're working with partners like a16z crypto, Utila, and Aegis. Each one brings something different to the ecosystem.

62493410
>>62491816
What's happening by October?

62494059
>>62492766
Something tells me we're only seeing part of the picture. Wouldn't be surprised if there's more to come.

62494064
>>62493410
Are we expecting bear market?

62494358
>>62475235
I already have a decent bitcoin position and will be dca'ing more whenever it's below the 200 week. It's lame and boring but it works. My guess is that it bottoms between the 200 and 400 week MAs.

>>62475258
Dunk on ben all you want he's mostly right most of the time.

62494434
>>62494358
I'm not dunking, his videos are great.

62494839
>>62494358
Boring works. If you want to do more with your BTC, native staking already exists. TBVs look worth checking out once they're live.

62494846
>>62490309
It's easy to throw out price targets. Explaining why bitcoin will actually get there is the hard part.

62495219
>>62494434
How great?

62495227
>>62489716
What are you thinking there joe

62495280
.

62495641
>>62480931
buy 2 btc

62495924
>>62475235
I'm buying more, If I eventually need liquidity, I might lend later when TBV is live
>>62475971
There will never be 10K

62496666
>>62495924
you sure about that? it might go zero ?

62496951
>>62495641
slowly

Take a look what it said about the stock market.

omg pls no

62497077

62497089
>>62497077

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62497097
>>62497077

it's trained on reddit posts -- of course that will be the outcome. normalgroids are spending their hard earned shekels on a judeo-indian black box that spits out amalgamated reddit posts. some normalgroids are even basing major life decisions on these "responses."

62497126
>>62496955

62497127

I'm expecting 70%, at least.

62497173

item.title: Why is there so much negativity surrounding the UK? — 115 replies — 8.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why is there so much negativity surrounding the UK?

Open thread • Replies: 115 • Updated: 2026-07-17 23:57 UTC

OP

Every time I see the UK mentioned here, it is always in a negative light.

It is always
>In the USA, wages are better!
>In Europe, houses are cheaper!
>The FTSE is underperforming!
>My taxes are too high!

There is never any mention of the fantastic benefits which come with life in the UK. The fantastic healthcare for minimal cost, the great education system available to everybody, the dynamic modern economy with many bustling sectors, and everything else.

Taxes are higher than the USA but they are lower than most of Europe and we still enjoy fantastic public services whereas in America there are no public services or safety nets.

Not to mention the things you cannot measure. Being able to walk the streets in safety without fear of gun violence and gangs. Enjoying the fantastic beautiful nature and gardens. Living in the historical majesty and balancing that with modern cosmopolitan qualities.

The UK might not be the best place to become rich but nobody is poor here and it is very easy to enjoy life if you have goals beyond the number in your bank account.

Early replies (first 3)

62481973
>>62481958
the problem is that its being filled with immigrants who are living off tax payers money
thats the gist of why everyone is so pissed off here

62481986
>>62481973
So just right wing racebaiting?

No, I think it runs deeper than that. Most people have not personally had negative experiences with migrants, and reading the Daily Mail does not count.

I am trying to angle from a financial perspective too. People talk about the UK like it is a poor country but it is one of the richest countries on the planet. I think perhaps they are just spoiled and do not know what poor countries are really like. I blame social media.

62481994
>>62481958
>fantastic healthcare for minimal cost
my nan lived in birmingham and she came down with leukaemia and was gone within a couple of months. She never even got to see a doctor because the muslim immigrants were getting seen first. if i had something to lose i wouldnt even dare post this for fear of legal reprimand.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62490030
>>62487539
We voted for sovereignty from unelected european bureaucrats. We voted to take Britain back and undo the mistakes we made under the EU.

>>62487523
Brexit is the best thing to happen to this country in decades. There are teething pains because europe refuses to accept us leaving and make deals with us but we are free to put the Great back into Britain.

62490071
>>62481958
Muhammad has been the most popular baby name for boys for 3 years in a row

62490585
A lot of it is shills/terminally online retards. The entire county I live is is 97% white (last census so probs a bit lower) and we've just bought a nice 400k house on a combined salary of 90k. I can and do walk/cycle around in full safety at any time of day. Maybe some smack head might look at me wrong but that's the worst of it. I would rather live here than America and most of Europe every day of the week

62490813
>>62490585
I'd ask where that is but I wouldn't want to blow it for you
Assuming its not a larp, godspeed anon

62490838
Sometimes I feel like responding to Europoors. Like I live in a capital city just minutes on foot to restaurants, shops, grocery stores. I pay less than 1k USD for my apartment. I can walk to work. I feel 100 percent safe, and could carry a firearm or knife if I so pleased but I am not a pussy. I often think about replying to Europoors and then I remember this meme.

62490850
>>62490585
Great post. A brit brangs about being able to walk/cycle outside and not getting stabbed.

62492082
>>62490850
bitter

62492388
>>62492082
Bitter about what?

62492412
>>62487400
>pictured: the entire UK fleet right before being crippled by their annual necessary repairs

62492424
>>62481973
>here
You need to get out before youre arrested for posting that

62492526
>>62492388
In context of the thread I was hardly bragging. I dare you to post where you live so we can have a go too

62492845
>>62492526
I live in a very expensive area created by corrupt political nepo insiders for corrupt political nepo insiders. Everybody has 2-3 newest Audi or mercedes SUVs and several millions in the bank. All the houses are 3,000+ square feet and have gardens. But not me, I'm an outsider here, and everybody knows that because they all know each other and that I'm not part of their club. All the neighbors are professional dogshit cunt parasites. So yeah you live in a better place.

62492892
>>62490838
>and could carry a firearm or knife

Love it how you're so compelled to mention this but are too afraid to use them for real because you'll be locked up.

Feel free to use it in a school or mall though. Seems like the average American pastime.

62492960
>>62492845
why would you move there? Sounds stupid

62493072
>>62481958
>Being able to walk the streets in safety without fear of gun violence and gangs.
You can neither walk nor talk safely.
It's a beautiful country, it WAS a nice culture otherwise.
Now, if you have a daughter, or are a fuckable woman, you will get raped if not tortured by some gang, if you complain you will be arrested for racism, if you protest you will get beaten by the same worthless bobbies who refused to help, and it's all part of the meanest large scale society destuction, ethnic replacement, power grab and money grab back to feudalism ever in European history.
The economy is not better.

62493208
>>62492960
Because I'm a poorfag retard, it's not my place and I can live here for free.

62493518
>>62493072
what a load of tripe. 'Pakistani grooming gangs' weren't actually abducting and raping women you stupid retard. They were grooming them

62493619
>>62493518
even if that isn't a lie, "oh jeez, that's so much better, thanks!". you fucking clown, kys

62493666
>>62481958
if it's so great, stay there. stop running around and shitting everywhere. you flag is gay, and youor people are ugly af

62493751
>>62490585
>97% white
If you think it will stay that way in the next 20 years, you're delusional.
>>62482110

62493775
>>62481958
Because it run by Israel and occupied by Palestine

62493802
>>62481958
>Why is there so much negativity surrounding the UK?
out of the starmer frying pan into the burnham fire

62493836
>>62481958

62493843
>>62493751
do you think where you live will stay the same for 20 years too?

62493991
>>62493843
No, but that's completely beside the point because I wasn't the one bragging about living in some ethno state paradise.

62494575
>>62482009
This is an unacceptable post and the police have been informed.

62494624
Slimes abducted, raped, tortured and had dogs rape 250k British girls. If you think this is ok, you are a pedo globalist, a Jew, or a pedo globalist Jew.

62495552
>>62493802
>46 years of capitalist exploitation
>widely accepted that the country has declined since then
>somehow, this is not capitalisms fault
>in fact, it's socialisms fault
>somehow
>even though the nordic countries have done really well under socialism
>and america is a warzone full of drugs and homeless people
Yes, what we need is MORE corporate exploitation. That wealth is just about to trickle down! We have been waiting since *checks notes* Reagan but it's just about to happen!

62495765
>>62481958
Uk is hell on earth. 2nd worst country in the world right after india

62495935
>>62482008
>>62482025
>>62484965
Lmao. The second the numbers look bad, suddenly metrics don't matter.

GDP per capita. Median income. Salaries. Productivity. They're all "bad metrics" now.

The nice thing about GDP, salary, height, BMI, IQ tests, or any other quantitative measure is that the number still exists whether it makes you seethe or not.

Our poorest state is richer than your entire country on a per-person basis. Your doctors earn what our gas station managers make. Kneel.

item.title: so when DTCC and Swift actually start using chainlink for their every day operations, wouldn't that mean the chainlink L… — 101 replies — 7.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
so when DTCC and Swift actually start using chainlink for their every day operations, wouldn't that mean the chainlink L…

Open thread • Replies: 101 • Updated: 2026-07-17 23:29 UTC

OP

so when DTCC and Swift actually start using chainlink for their every day operations, wouldn't that mean the chainlink LLC would be making boatloads of cash from the fees? And wouldn't these fees be used to buy chainlink on the open market, thus impacting the price positively?

Early replies (first 3)

62476682
>>62476675
He is not necessary.

62476690
>>62476675
your logic is absolutely correct. This will result in BTC dumping like its life depends on it.

62476730
>>62476675
Inb4 tranny anime poster

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62481761
>>62481678
it's not like that at all
marvel and DC are both successful franchises
xrp is just a scam

62482151
>>62481749
You're talking on fucking riddles you retard. I don't even know who xerp is.
I've literally just debunked all of your claims and now you're making shit up

62482524
>>62479364
>XRP
BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM SHAKALA. I am new to 4chan but have been loving this meme lately! I read on reddit yesterday that this would happen and I boughted (but unfortunately the price basically immediately soon after). I was still so pumped I went to Costco and got a slice of pizza and sat facing those walking out looking at everyone walking by knowing and rye-ly smiling knowing none of them understood what is happening. I felt like Brownstone Capital with Ben Rickerts in the Big Short. I had to stop myself from celebrating because I remembered they were missing out and I wanted to feel the same type of compassion they were reminded to feel by Ben in that moment so I finished my pizza solemnly leaving a bite out of clenched stomach respect for them not being able to finish my meal, grabbed my bag, put my hood up, and walked out nodding to the door inspector knowing looking at them knowingly and humanly as I walked out with them smiling not knowing beneath my shades how I was really seeing them. It was an amazing experience and it felt like everyone was watching but I know they weren't because the thing that made it special was that they don't understand or see. I will come back and tip the kitchen staff handsomely (especially the girl who I like but have not had the courage to ask out....yet) when we are making bookoo bucks and I can afford to own the whole store and promote her. and then smirk as I tell her I will sell the store, non-chalantly, so that I can ask her so she would feel comfortable if I ask her out on a date after I made her general manager of the store. I can't wait!

62483155
>>62482524
Anyone reading this linkie written post, for your own entertainment also see:

62483160

62483496
I think it depends on how the fee model actually works. Institutional adoption doesn't automatically mean constant buy pressure unless part of the revenue is consistently tied to acquiring LINK from the open market. I'd wait to see how the economics play out instead of assuming usage alone guarantees price appreciation. I'm also interested in how Candora Agent will help analyze market events and sentiment once it launches.

62487489
>>62484352
>Theyre not gonna share that revenue with you
Chainlink revenue is shared with Link holders because more demand for Chainlink products directly translates to more demand for the Link token.
Because CL nodes must be paid in Link for literally everything they do, and there isn't a single Chainlink product, service, or feature that does not involve CL nodes.

If you wanna talk about revenue issues, the absolute worst mismatch I can think of in the crypto space is happening at Ripple.
They actively dump XRP and use that money to buy companies that don't use XRP at all but that boost revenue for Ripple shareholders.
